raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need diff and count lines that differ. Use diff -u and wc -l? Actually count differing lines: diff -u file1 file2 | grep -c '^+'? But diff output includes context. Use diff -u file1 file2 | grep -c '^+'? But that counts added lines. For differing lines both added and removed. Use diff -u file1 file2 | grep -c '^[-+]'? But diff output includes lines starting with '-' or '+', but also lines starting with '---' and '+++'. So exclude those. Use grep -c '^[+-][^-+]'? Actually lines starting with '-' or '+' but not '---' or '+++'.

Command: diff -u file1 file2 | grep -c '^[+-][^-+]'? Wait '^[+-][^-+]' means first char is + or -, second char not - or +. That excludes '---' and '+++'.

So final: diff -u /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | grep -c '^[+-][^-+]'
